patch.value: Structured remediation map on pm health --json for all non-extension checks

Agents consuming pm health --json get warning codes (history_drift_missing_stream, integrity_item_unreadable, telemetry_queue_pending, vectorization_stale) but must hardcode the fix; only extensions return triage.remediation.

Each check details gains remediation_map (warning-code-prefix -> fix command) e.g. history_drift->pm history-repair <id>, vectorization->pm health --refresh-vectors; brief omits it; full includes it; test asserts a non-empty map.

Agents consuming pm health --json get warning codes (history_drift_missing_stream, integrity_item_unreadable, telemetry_queue_pending, vectorization_stale) but must hardcode the fix; only extensions return triage.remediation.

Each check detail gains remediation_map (warning-code-prefix -> fix command) e.g. history_drift->pm history-repair <id>, vectorization->pm health --refresh-vectors; brief omits it; full includes it; test asserts a non-empty map.

author: maintainer-agent
message: Cluster: agent self-repair via shared remediation registry (health --json remediation_map + validate --fix-hints + settings warning surfacing)

patch.value: Added details.remediation_map (warning-code-prefix -> executable pm fix command) to every non-extension health check via the shared registry src/core/diagnostics/remediation.ts; brief/summary omit it; extensions keep details.triage.remediation.
Agents read a fix command per warning code from pm health --json without hardcoding mappings.
history_drift/vectorization/integrity/settings/telemetry/directories checks now carry remediation_map when they warn; verified in sandbox.
Shipped per-check remediation_map on pm health --json
